stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking failures:

- REQ-02 is not met. Qualified ids are `<issue>/<self-id>`, but uniqueness is only enforced per kind, not per issue across all kinds. In [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:348), `seen` is recreated inside the `for kind in kinds` loop, and the duplicate check at [line 362](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:362) only catches duplicates within that one kind. Since `qualified_id` at [line 371](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:371) does not include kind, two configured kinds can produce the same `<issue>/REQ-01`. `show_item` then returns the first matching self-id at [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:168](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:168), making resolution ambiguous.

- REQ-05 is not met. The requirement says generic link namespaces reference qualified ids like `<ns>:<issue>/<self-id>`, but the implementation only demonstrates compatibility with the existing unqualified `satisfies:REQ-01` shape. The new harness test adds `satisfies:REQ-01` at [crates/jit/tests/harness_demo.rs:307](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/harness_demo.rs:307), and no validation/rule path was updated to accept or resolve `satisfies:<issue>/<self-id>`. The namespace example in `.jit/config.toml` is still unqualified (`satisfies:REQ-01`).

- The issue concept explicitly calls out resolving `jit show <issue>/<self-id>`, but the change only wires `jit item show/resolve`; `Commands::Item` is handled at [crates/jit/src/main.rs:4051](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:4051). Existing `issue show` dispatch was not extended for qualified item ids.

REQ-01, REQ-03, REQ-04 list/search basics, and REQ-06 are partially implemented: config parsing exists, projection is derived, item CLI commands exist, and prose without ids is skipped.

**Architecture**

Layering is mostly correct. The projection is in [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:339), repository reads go through `IssueStore` in [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:70), and CLI rendering stays in `main.rs`. No direct file access was introduced outside storage for the item feature.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue here. The new command/domain code uses some imperative loops in [domain/item.rs:349](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:349) and [commands/item.rs:75](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:75), but the logic remains pure in domain and side effects remain at command/storage boundaries.

**Correctness And Invariants**

No new writes are introduced by the item query feature, so atomic write and event logging requirements are not directly affected. Gate semantics, assignee format, labels format, and PID safety are unchanged.

Blocking correctness issue is the duplicate qualified-id invariant described above.

**Safety**

`#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains present in [crates/jit/src/lib.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/lib.rs:6) and [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:14). I found no new unsafe code in this change range.

**Testing**

I did not run the test suite because the issue has a succeeded `cargo-ci` gate (`passed`, 2026-06-25 13:17:55 UTC). `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

Coverage gap: there is no test for cross-kind self-id collisions producing duplicate qualified ids. The existing duplicate test only covers duplicate ids within the default requirement kind at [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:486](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:486). There is also no CLI test proving `--json` error output for item commands.

**Error Handling**

Blocking CLI/error contract issue: `jit item ... --json` failures are not rendered as JSON. `run_item` propagates errors with `?` at [crates/jit/src/main.rs:444](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:444) and [line 453](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:453); the top-level `main` prints plain `Error: ...` at [crates/jit/src/main.rs:603](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:603). That violates the machine-readable `--json` contract for failing item commands.

**CLI Contract**

Success JSON is implemented for `item list/show/search/resolve`. Error JSON is not implemented for those commands, as noted above. The broader qualified-id resolution behavior expected by the issue (`jit show <issue>/<self-id>`) is also missing.

**Documentation**

Public APIs added for item kinds and item commands generally have doc comments and examples. No blocking documentation issue found.

**Prior Review Feedback**

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior feedback to verify.

**Jit Issue Management**

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed. The listed dependency `5f5735c3` is marked `done`, so prerequisite state is satisfied. Label warnings were not observed in the validation run.

VERDICT: FAIL
